diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient presented with a right distal humerus fracture after tripping and falling while using a walker. X-rays confirmed an angulated, displaced distal humerus fracture, while head, C-spine, bilateral knee, chest, and pelvis x-rays were negative for trauma. The absence of pain, numbness, weakness, headache, neck pain, back pain, chest pain, shortness of breath, or abdominal pain further supported the diagnosis.
